revision.text:
{{Sakujo/本体|2015年9月14日|但馬国の式内社記事}} {{Copyright}} {{神社 |名称 = 雷神社 |画像 = [[File:668-300x225.jpg|thumb|雷神社拝殿・本殿]] |所在地 = [[兵庫県]][[豊岡市]]佐野542-2 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|30|45.14|N|134|48|33.11|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |主祭神 = [[オホイカツチノカミ|大雷神]] |社格 = [[式内社]]（（名神大）[[名神大社|名神大]]）
旧[[村社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = [[切妻造]]五間社　三間社向拝付　桧皮葺 ||例祭 = [[5月1日]] |神事 = }} [[File:661-300x225.jpg|thumb|雷神社境内]] '''雷神社'''（いかづちじんじゃ）は、兵庫県豊岡市にある神社。式内社（名神大社）、旧社格は村社。佐野天神とも称した。 == 概要 == 創建年代は不詳だが、『国司文書　但馬神社系譜伝』　人皇四十二代文武天皇の慶雲三年（707）七月と記されている。 但馬国の名神大社十社中の一社に数えられた古社。主祭神の大雷神は、またの名を火雷神、 別雷神と申し、雨乞い、稲作の守護神。配祀神に須佐之男命・菅原道真を祀る。菅原道真は死して天神（雷の神）になったと伝えられる。 == 祭神 == [オホイカツチノカミ|大[[雷神]] 『国司文書・但馬神社系譜伝』には、（[[火雷神]]、またの名[[別雷神]]） == 文化財 == *雷神社本殿（県指定文化財） == 歴史 == 『続日本後紀』承和9年10月乙亥（15日）条（842） 但馬国気多郡山神。雷神。戸神。蜀椒神。城埼郡海神等五前。並預二官社一。 『日本三代実録』貞観10年12月27日丙戌条（868） 授二但馬国従五位上出石神。粟鹿神並正五位下。従五位下山神。戸神。雷神。[木蜀]椒神。海神並従五位上一。 『延喜式』3（臨時祭）・名神祭条〔28〕 粟鹿神社一座。夜夫神社二座。伊豆志神社八座。 山神社一座。戸神社一座。雷神社一座。 [木蜀]椒神社一座。海神社一座。已上但馬国。 == 但馬国官幣社について == 『延喜式』3（臨時祭）・名神祭条〔28〕 名神祭二百八十五座。粟鹿神社一座。夜夫神社二座。伊豆志神社八座。 山神社一座。戸神社一座。雷神社一座。 [木蜀]椒神社一座。海神社一座。已上但馬国。 『国司文書　但馬神社系譜伝』 人皇四十二代文武天皇の慶雲三年七月、 丹波・但馬二国山災となる。 使を遣わし、神祀に幣帛を賜る。 すなわち雷声弊に応じ、たちまち消滅を忍ばす。よって雷神を狭沼丘に祀り、長く雷災の免れることを祈る。 末社 == == 祭事 == == 現地情報 == ; 交通アクセス ''バス'' * [[豊岡駅]]（JR西日本山陰本線）から、全但バス納屋バス停下車、約24分、徒歩1.4km、23分 ''車'' * 国道312号線但馬空港入口交差点から空港方面に北西へ約1.8km、約24分 ; 周辺 *こうのとり但馬空港 *豊岡鞄団地 ; 関連項目 *[[但馬国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322256.html]（兵庫県神社庁） *[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/380301.html]（國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム　神社史料集成） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:いかづちじんじゃ}} [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:豊岡市]] [[Category:豊岡市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:但馬国の式内社|名]] [[Category:但馬国|社]] [[Category:気多郡 (兵庫県)]]

旧[[村社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = [[切妻造]]五間社　三間社向拝付　桧皮葺 ||例祭 = [[5月1日]] |神事 = }} [[File:661-300x225.jpg|thumb|雷神社境内]] '''雷神社'''（いかづちじんじゃ）は、兵庫県豊岡市にある神社。式内社（名神大社）、旧社格は村社。佐野天神とも称した。 == 概要 == 創建年代は不詳だが、『国司文書　但馬神社系譜伝』　人皇四十二代文武天皇の慶雲三年（707）七月と記されている。 但馬国の名神大社十社中の一社に数えられた古社。主祭神の大雷神は、またの名を火雷神、 別雷神と申し、雨乞い、稲作の守護神。配祀神に須佐之男命・菅原道真を祀る。菅原道真は死して天神（雷の神）になったと伝えられる。 == 祭神 == [オホイカツチノカミ|大[[雷神]] 『国司文書・但馬神社系譜伝』には、（[[火雷神]]、またの名[[別雷神]]） == 文化財 == *雷神社本殿（県指定文化財） == 歴史 == 『続日本後紀』承和9年10月乙亥（15日）条（842） 但馬国気多郡山神。雷神。戸神。蜀椒神。城埼郡海神等五前。並預二官社一。 『日本三代実録』貞観10年12月27日丙戌条（868） 授二但馬国従五位上出石神。粟鹿神並正五位下。従五位下山神。戸神。雷神。[木蜀]椒神。海神並従五位上一。 『延喜式』3（臨時祭）・名神祭条〔28〕 粟鹿神社一座。夜夫神社二座。伊豆志神社八座。 山神社一座。戸神社一座。雷神社一座。 [木蜀]椒神社一座。海神社一座。已上但馬国。 == 但馬国官幣社について == 『延喜式』3（臨時祭）・名神祭条〔28〕 名神祭二百八十五座。粟鹿神社一座。夜夫神社二座。伊豆志神社八座。 山神社一座。戸神社一座。雷神社一座。 [木蜀]椒神社一座。海神社一座。已上但馬国。 『国司文書　但馬神社系譜伝』 人皇四十二代文武天皇の慶雲三年七月、 丹波・但馬二国山災となる。 使を遣わし、神祀に幣帛を賜る。 すなわち雷声弊に応じ、たちまち消滅を忍ばす。よって雷神を狭沼丘に祀り、長く雷災の免れることを祈る。 末社 == == 祭事 == == 現地情報 == ; 交通アクセス ''バス'' * [[豊岡駅 (兵庫県)|豊岡駅]]（JR西日本山陰本線）から、全但バス納屋バス停下車、約24分、徒歩1.4km、23分 ''車'' * 国道312号線但馬空港入口交差点から空港方面に北西へ約1.8km、約24分 ; 周辺 * こうのとり但馬空港 * 豊岡鞄団地 ; 関連項目 * [[但馬国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322256.html]（兵庫県神社庁） *[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/380301.html]（國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム　神社史料集成） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:いかづちじんじゃ}} [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:豊岡市]] [[Category:豊岡市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:但馬国の式内社|名]] [[Category:但馬国|社]] [[Category:気多郡 (兵庫県)]]

旧[[村社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = [[切妻造]]五間社　三間社向拝付　桧皮葺 |例祭 = [[5月1日]] |神事 = |地図 = Japan Hyogo }} [[File:661-300x225.jpg|thumb|雷神社境内]] '''雷神社'''（いかづちじんじゃ）は、兵庫県豊岡市にある神社。式内社（名神大社）、旧社格は村社。佐野天神とも称した。 == 概要 == 創建年代は不詳だが、『国司文書　但馬神社系譜伝』　人皇四十二代文武天皇の慶雲三年（707）七月と記されている。 但馬国の名神大社十社中の一社に数えられた古社。主祭神の大雷神は、またの名を火雷神、 別雷神と申し、雨乞い、稲作の守護神。配祀神に須佐之男命・菅原道真を祀る。菅原道真は死して天神（雷の神）になったと伝えられる。 == 祭神 == 大[[雷神]]（オホイカツチノカミ） 『国司文書・但馬神社系譜伝』には、（[[火雷神]]、またの名[[別雷神]]） == 文化財 == *雷神社本殿（県指定文化財） == 歴史 == 『続日本後紀』承和9年10月乙亥（15日）条（842） 但馬国気多郡山神。雷神。戸神。蜀椒神。城埼郡海神等五前。並預二官社一。 『日本三代実録』貞観10年12月27日丙戌条（868） 授二但馬国従五位上出石神。粟鹿神並正五位下。従五位下山神。戸神。雷神。[木蜀]椒神。海神並従五位上一。 『延喜式』3（臨時祭）・名神祭条〔28〕 粟鹿神社一座。夜夫神社二座。伊豆志神社八座。 山神社一座。戸神社一座。雷神社一座。 [木蜀]椒神社一座。海神社一座。已上但馬国。 == 但馬国官幣社について == 『延喜式』3（臨時祭）・名神祭条〔28〕 名神祭二百八十五座。粟鹿神社一座。夜夫神社二座。伊豆志神社八座。 山神社一座。戸神社一座。雷神社一座。 [木蜀]椒神社一座。海神社一座。已上但馬国。 『国司文書　但馬神社系譜伝』 人皇四十二代文武天皇の慶雲三年七月、 丹波・但馬二国山災となる。 使を遣わし、神祀に幣帛を賜る。 すなわち雷声弊に応じ、たちまち消滅を忍ばす。よって雷神を狭沼丘に祀り、長く雷災の免れることを祈る。 == 末社 == == 祭事 == == 現地情報 == ; 交通アクセス ''バス'' * [[豊岡駅 (兵庫県)|豊岡駅]]（JR西日本山陰本線）から、全但バス納屋バス停下車、約24分、徒歩1.4km、23分 ''車'' * 国道312号線但馬空港入口交差点から空港方面に北西へ約1.8km、約24分 ; 周辺 * こうのとり但馬空港 * 豊岡鞄団地 == 関連項目 == * [[但馬国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322053.html 雷神社]（兵庫県神社庁） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:いかつちしんしや}} [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:豊岡市]] [[Category:豊岡市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:但馬国の式内社|名]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧村社]]

revision.comment: 埼玉県熊谷市上之鎮座。雷神社から勧請された大雷神をお祀りしている神社です。

{{神社 |名称 = 雷神社 |画像 = [[File:668-300x225.jpg|270px|雷神社拝殿・本殿]] |所在地 = [[兵庫県]][[豊岡市]]佐野542番地2 |ISO =JP-28 | 緯度度 = 35 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 45.1 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 48 | 経度秒 = 33.1 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |祭神 = 大[[雷神]]（オホイカツチノカミ） |社格 = [[式内社]]（[[名神大社|名神大]]）
旧[[村社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = [[切妻造]]五間社　三間社向拝付　桧皮葺 |例祭 = [[5月1日]] |神事 = |地図 = Japan Hyogo }} [[File:661-300x225.jpg|thumb|雷神社境内]] '''雷神社'''（いかづちじんじゃ）は、[[兵庫県]][[豊岡市]]にある神社。式内社（名神大社）、旧社格は村社。佐野天神とも称した。 == 概要 == 創建年代は不詳。大雷神を主祭神とし、配祀神に[[須佐之男命]]・[[菅原道真]]を祀る。菅原道真は死して天神（雷の神）になったと伝えられる。 == 祭神 == *[[八柱雷神|大雷神]] *[[須佐之男命]] *[[菅原道真]] == 文化財 == *雷神社本殿（県指定文化財） == 歴史 == 『続日本後紀』承和9年10月乙亥（15日）条（842） 但馬国気多郡山神。雷神。戸神。蜀椒神。城埼郡海神等五前。並預二官社一。 『日本三代実録』貞観10年12月27日丙戌条（868） 授二但馬国従五位上出石神。粟鹿神並正五位下。従五位下山神。戸神。雷神。[木蜀]椒神。海神並従五位上一。 『延喜式』3（臨時祭）・名神祭条〔28〕 粟鹿神社一座。夜夫神社二座。伊豆志神社八座。 山神社一座。戸神社一座。雷神社一座。 [木蜀]椒神社一座。海神社一座。已上但馬国。 == 但馬国官幣社について == 『延喜式』3（臨時祭）・名神祭条〔28〕 名神祭二百八十五座。粟鹿神社一座。夜夫神社二座。伊豆志神社八座。 山神社一座。戸神社一座。雷神社一座。 [木蜀]椒神社一座。海神社一座。已上但馬国。 『国司文書　但馬神社系譜伝』 人皇四十二代文武天皇の慶雲三年七月、 丹波・但馬二国山災となる。 使を遣わし、神祀に幣帛を賜る。 すなわち雷声弊に応じ、たちまち消滅を忍ばす。よって雷神を狭沼丘に祀り、長く雷災の免れることを祈る。 == 末社 == == 祭事 == == 現地情報 == ; 交通アクセス ''バス'' * [[豊岡駅 (兵庫県)|豊岡駅]]（JR西日本山陰本線）から、全但バス納屋バス停下車、約24分、徒歩1.4km、23分 ''車'' * 国道312号線但馬空港入口交差点から空港方面に北西へ約1.8km、約24分 ; 周辺 * こうのとり但馬空港 * 豊岡鞄団地 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}} == 関連項目 == * [[但馬国の式内社一覧]] * [[上之村神社|上之 雷電神社（上之村神社）]] == 外部リンク == *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322053.html 雷神社]（兵庫県神社庁） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:いかつちしんしや}} [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:豊岡市の建築物]] [[Category:豊岡市の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:但馬国の式内社|名]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧村社]]

revision.text:
[[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]]
'''Ikazuchi Shrine''' (雷神社, Ikazuchi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Hyōgo Prefecture. It enshrines the {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神}} and is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} system. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine (村社) under the Modern Shrine Ranking System and is also referred to as Sano Tenjin (佐野天神).
== Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (菅原道真). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable additional deity.
== Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}
== Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property.
== History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9年10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10年12月27日, 868): Grants various ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province.
According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters.
== Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}.
== Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from {{ill|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)|ja|豊岡駅}} (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes).
''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312.
== Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地)
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine).
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322053.html Ikazuchi Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Bureau)
{{Shinto Horizontal}}

[[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]]
'''Ikazuchi Shrine''' (雷神社, Ikazuchi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Hyōgo Prefecture. It enshrines the {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神}} and is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} system. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine (村社) under the Modern Shrine Ranking System and is also referred to as Sano Tenjin (佐野天神).
== Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (菅原道真). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable additional deity.
== Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}
== Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property.
== History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9年10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10年12月27日, 868): Grants various ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province.
According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters.
== Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}.
== Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from {{ill|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)|ja|豊岡駅}} (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes).
''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312.
== Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地)
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine).

It enshrines the {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神}} and is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} system. ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322053.html Ikazuchi Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Bureau) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ikazuchi Shrine}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyōgo]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Prefectural Cultural Properties in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[ja:雷神社 (豊岡市)]]

[[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] {{Shrine | Name = Raijin Shrine | Image = [[File:668-300x225.jpg|270px|Raijin Shrine worship hall and main hall]] | Location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Sano 542-2 | ISO = JP-28 | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 45.1 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 134 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 33.1 | E or W = E | Deity = {{ill|Ohikatsuji-no-Kami|ja|オホイカツチノカミ}} (Great Thunder God) | Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}})
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | Established = Unknown | Main hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}} (five-bay shrine, three-bay worship hall, hinoki-bark roof) | Annual festival = {{ill|May 1|ja|5月1日}} | Rituals = | Map = Japan Hyogo }} '''Ikazuchi Shrine''' (雷神社, Ikazuchi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Hyōgo Prefecture. It enshrines the {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神}} and is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} system. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine (村社) under the Modern Shrine Ranking System and is also referred to as Sano Tenjin (佐野天神). == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (菅原道真). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable additional deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9年10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10年12月27日, 868): Grants various ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from {{ill|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)|ja|豊岡駅}} (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322053.html Ikazuchi Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Bureau) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ikazuchi Shrine}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyōgo]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Prefectural Cultural Properties in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[ja:雷神社 (豊岡市)]]

[[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] {{Shrine | Name = Raijin Shrine | Image = [[File:668-300x225.jpg|270px|Raijin Shrine worship hall and main hall]] | Location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Sano 542-2 | ISO = JP-28 | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 45.1 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 134 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 33.1 | E or W = E | Deity = {{ill|Ohikatsuji-no-Kami|ja|オホイカツチノカミ}} (Great Thunder God) | Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}})
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | Established = Unknown | Main hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}} (five-bay shrine, three-bay worship hall, hinoki-bark roof) | Annual festival = {{ill|May 1|ja|5月1日}} | Rituals = | Map = Japan Hyogo }} '''Ikazuchi Shrine''' (雷神社, Ikazuchi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Hyōgo Prefecture. It enshrines the {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神}} and is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} system. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine (村社) under the Modern Shrine Ranking System and is also referred to as Sano Tenjin (佐野天神). == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (菅原道真). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable additional deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9年10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10年12月27日, 868): Grants various ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from {{ill|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)|ja|豊岡駅}} (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322053.html Ikazuchi Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Bureau) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ikazuchi Shrine}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyōgo]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Prefectural Cultural Properties in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[ja:雷神社 (豊岡市)]]

It enshrines the {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神}} and is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} system. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine (村社) under the Modern Shrine Ranking System and is also referred to as Sano Tenjin (佐野天神). == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (菅原道真). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable additional deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9年10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10年12月27日, 868): Grants various ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from {{ill|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)|ja|豊岡駅}} (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322053.html Ikazuchi Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Bureau) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ikazuchi Shrine}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyōgo]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Prefectural Cultural Properties in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[ja:雷神社 (豊岡市)]]

Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable additional deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9年10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10年12月27日, 868): Grants various ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from {{ill|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)|ja|豊岡駅}} (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from {{ill|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)|ja|豊岡駅}} (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). [[Category:dd]][[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] {{Shrine | name = Raijin Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:668-300x225.jpg|270px|Raijin Shrine worship hall and main hall]] | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Sano 542-2 | ISO = JP-28 | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 45.1 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 134 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 33.1 | E or W = E | Deity = {{ill|Ohikatsuji-no-Kami|ja|オホイカツチノカミ}} (Great Thunder God) | Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}})
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | Established = Unknown | Main hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}} (five-bay shrine, three-bay worship hall, hinoki-bark roof) | Annual festival = {{ill|May 1|ja|5月1日}} | Rituals = | Map = Japan Hyogo }} '''Ikazuchi Shrine''' (雷神社, Ikazuchi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Hyōgo Prefecture. It enshrines the {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神}} and is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} system. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine (村社) under the Modern typeing System and is also referred to as Sano Tenjin (佐野天神). == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (菅原道真). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable additional deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9年10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10年12月27日, 868): Grants various ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from {{ill|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)|ja|豊岡駅}} (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322053.html Ikazuchi Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Bureau) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ikazuchi Shrine}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyōgo]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Prefectural Cultural Properties in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[ja:雷神社 (豊岡市)]]

[[Category:dd]][[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] {{Shrine | name = Raijin Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:668-300x225.jpg|270px|Raijin Shrine worship hall and main hall]] | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Sano 542-2 | ISO = JP-28 | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 45.1 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 134 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 33.1 | E or W = E | Deity = {{ill|Ohikatsuji-no-Kami|ja|オホイカツチノカミ}} (Great Thunder God) | Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}})
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | Established = Unknown | Main hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}} (five-bay shrine, three-bay worship hall, hinoki-bark roof) | Annual festival = {{ill|May 1|ja|5月1日}} | Rituals = | Map = Japan Hyogo }} '''Ikazuchi Shrine''' (雷神社, Ikazuchi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Hyōgo Prefecture. It enshrines the {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神}} and is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} system. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine (村社) under the Modern typeing System and is also referred to as Sano Tenjin (佐野天神). == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (菅原道真). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable additional deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9年10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10年12月27日, 868): Grants various ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from {{ill|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)|ja|豊岡駅}} (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322053.html Ikazuchi Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Bureau) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ikazuchi Shrine}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322053.html Ikazuchi Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Bureau) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ikazuchi Shrine}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]][[Category:Toyooka, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]][[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]][[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]][[Category:Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]][[Category:Prefectural Cultural Properties in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:雷神社 (豊岡市)]]

[[Category:dd]][[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] {{Shrine | name = Raijin Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:668-300x225.jpg|270px|Raijin Shrine worship hall and main hall]] | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Sano 542-2 | ISO = JP-28 | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 45.1 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 134 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 33.1 | E or W = E | deity = {{ill|Ohikatsuji-no-Kami|ja|オホイカツチノカミ}} (Great Thunder God) | Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}})
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | Established = Unknown | Main hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}} (five-bay shrine, three-bay worship hall, hinoki-bark roof) | Annual festival = {{ill|May 1|ja|5月1日}} | Rituals = | Map = Japan Hyogo }} '''Ikazuchi Shrine''' (雷神社, Ikazuchi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Hyōgo Prefecture. It enshrines the {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin}} and is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} system. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine (村社) under the Modern typeing System and is also referred to as Sano Tenjin (佐野天神). == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (菅原道真). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable additional deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9年10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10年12月27日, 868): Grants various ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from {{ill|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)|ja|豊岡駅}} (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322053.html Ikazuchi Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Bureau) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ikazuchi Shrine}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]][[Category:Toyooka, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]][[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]][[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]][[Category:Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]][[Category:Prefectural Cultural Properties in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:雷神社 (豊岡市)]]

[[Category:dd]][[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] {{Shrine | name = Raijin Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:668-300x225.jpg|270px|Raijin Shrine worship hall and main hall]] | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Sano 542-2 | ISO = JP-28 | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 45.1 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 134 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 33.1 | E or W = E | deity = {{ill|Ohikatsuji-no-Kami|ja|オホイカツチノカミ}} (Great Thunder God) | Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}})
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | Established = Unknown | Main hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}} (five-bay shrine, three-bay worship hall, hinoki-bark roof) | Annual festival = {{ill|May 1|ja|5月1日}} | Rituals = | Map = Japan Hyogo }} '''Ikazuchi Shrine''' (雷神社, Ikazuchi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Hyōgo Prefecture. It enshrines the {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin}} and is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} system. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine (村社) under the Modern typeing System and is also referred to as Sano Tenjin (佐野天神). == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (菅原道真). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable additional deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9年10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10年12月27日, 868): Grants various ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from {{ill|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)|ja|豊岡駅}} (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (菅原道真). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable additional deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9年10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10年12月27日, 868): Grants various ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from {{ill|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)|ja|豊岡駅}} (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港}} * Toyooka Bag District The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine (村社) under the Modern typeing System and is also referred to as Sano Tenjin (佐野天神). == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (菅原道真). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable additional deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9年10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10年12月27日, 868): Grants various ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from {{ill|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)|ja|豊岡駅}} (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == [[Category:dd]][[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Raijin Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:668-300x225.jpg|270px|Raijin Shrine worship hall and main hall]] | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Sano 542-2 | ISO = JP-28 | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 45.1 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 134 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 33.1 | E or W = E | deity = {{ill|Ohikatsuji-no-Kami|ja|オホイカツチノカミ}} (Great Thunder God) | Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}})
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | Established = Unknown | Main hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}} (five-bay shrine, three-bay worship hall, hinoki-bark roof) | Annual festival = {{ill|May 1|ja|5月1日}} | Rituals = | Map = Japan Hyogo }} '''Ikazuchi Shrine''' (雷神社, Ikazuchi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Hyōgo Prefecture. It enshrines the {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin}} and is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} system. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine (村社) under the Modern typeing System and is also referred to as Sano Tenjin (佐野天神). == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (菅原道真). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable additional deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9年10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10年12月27日, 868): Grants various ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from {{ill|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)|ja|豊岡駅}} (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == [[Category:dd]][[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Raijin Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:668-300x225.jpg|270px|Raijin Shrine worship hall and main hall]] | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Sano 542-2 | ISO = JP-28 | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 45.1 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 134 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 33.1 | E or W = E | deity = {{ill|Ohikatsuji-no-Kami|ja|オホイカツチノカミ}} (Great Thunder God) | Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}})
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | Established = Unknown | Main hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}} (five-bay shrine, three-bay worship hall, hinoki-bark roof) | Annual festival = {{ill|May 1|ja|5月1日}} | Rituals = | Map = Japan Hyogo }} '''Ikazuchi Shrine''' (雷神社, Ikazuchi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Hyōgo Prefecture. It enshrines the {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin}} and is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} system. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine (村社) under the Modern typeing System and is also referred to as Sano Tenjin (佐野天神). == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (菅原道真). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable additional deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9年10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10年12月27日, 868): Grants various ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from {{ill|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)|ja|豊岡駅}} (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322053.html Ikazuchi Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Bureau) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ikazuchi Shrine}} [[Category:Unsorted According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from {{ill|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)|ja|豊岡駅}} (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == [[Category:dd]][[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Raijin Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:668-300x225.jpg|270px|Raijin Shrine worship hall and main hall]] | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Sano 542-2 | ISO = JP-28 | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 45.1 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 134 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 33.1 | E or W = E | deity = {{ill|Ohikatsuji-no-Kami|ja|オホイカツチノカミ}} (Great Thunder God) | Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}})
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | Established = Unknown | Main hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}} (five-bay shrine, three-bay worship hall, hinoki-bark roof) | Annual festival = {{ill|May 1|ja|5月1日}} | Rituals = | Map = Japan Hyogo }} '''Ikazuchi Shrine''' (雷神社, Ikazuchi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Hyōgo Prefecture. It enshrines the {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin}} and is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} system. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine (村社) under the Modern typeing System and is also referred to as Sano Tenjin (佐野天神). == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (菅原道真). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable additional deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9年10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10年12月27日, 868): Grants various ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from {{ill|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)|ja|豊岡駅}} (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322053.html Ikazuchi Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Bureau) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ikazuchi Shrine}} [[Category:Unsorted The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (菅原道真). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable aqqitional deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9年10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10年12月27日, 868): Grants various ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from {{ill|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)|ja|豊岡駅}} (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[Category:qq]][[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Raijin Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:668-300x225.jpg|270px|Raijin Shrine worship hall and main hall]] | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Sano 542-2 | ISO = JP-28 | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 45.1 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 134 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 33.1 | E or W = E | deity = {{ill|Ohikatsuji-no-Kami|ja|オホイカツチノカミ}} (Great Thunder God) | Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}})
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | Established = Unknown | Main hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}} (five-bay shrine, three-bay worship hall, hinoki-bark roof) | Annual festival = {{ill|May 1|ja|5月1日}} | Rituals = | Map = Japan Hyogo }} '''Ikazuchi Shrine''' (雷神社, Ikazuchi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Hyōgo Prefecture. It enshrines the {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin}} and is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} system. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine (村社) under the Modern typeing System and is also referred to as Sano Tenjin (佐野天神). == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (菅原道真). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable aqqitional deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9年10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10年12月27日, 868): Grants various ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from {{ill|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)|ja|豊岡駅}} (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322053.html Ikazuchi Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Bureau) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ikazuchi Shrine}} [[Category:Unsorted revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[Category:qq]][[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Raijin Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:668-300x225.jpg|270px|Raijin Shrine worship hall and main hall]] | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Sano 542-2 | ISO = JP-28 | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 45.1 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 134 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 33.1 | E or W = E | deity = {{ill|Ohikatsuji-no-Kami|ja|オホイカツチノカミ}} (Great Thunder God) | Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}})
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | Established = Unknown | Main hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}} (five-bay shrine, three-bay worship hall, hinoki-bark roof) | Annual festival = {{ill|May 1|ja|5月1日}} | Rituals = | Map = Japan Hyogo }} '''Ikazuchi Shrine''' (雷神社, Ikazuchi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Hyōgo Prefecture. It enshrines the {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin}} and is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} system. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine (村社) under the Modern typeing System and is also referred to as Sano Tenjin (佐野天神). == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (菅原道真). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable aqqitional deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9年10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10年12月27日, 868): Grants various ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from {{ill|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)|ja|豊岡駅}} (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322053.html Ikazuchi Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Bureau) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ikazuchi Shrine}} [[Category:Unsorted Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from {{ill|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)|ja|豊岡駅}} (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == [[Category:qq]][[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Raijin Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:668-300x225.jpg|270px|Raijin Shrine worship hall and main hall]] | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Sano 542-2 | ISO = JP-28 | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 45.1 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 134 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 33.1 | E or W = E | deity = {{ill|Ohikatsuji-no-Kami|ja|オホイカツチノカミ}} (Great Thunder God) | Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}})
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | Established = Unknown | Main hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}} (five-bay shrine, three-bay worship hall, hinoki-bark roof) | Annual festival = {{ill|May 1|ja|5月1日}} | Rituals = | Map = Japan Hyogo }} '''Ikazuchi Shrine''' (雷神社, Ikazuchi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Hyōgo Prefecture. It enshrines the {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin}} and is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} system. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine (村社) under the Modern typeing System and is also referred to as Sano Tenjin (佐野天神). == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (菅原道真). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable aqqitional deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9年10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10年12月27日, 868): Grants various ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from {{ill|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)|ja|豊岡駅}} (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). [[Category:qq]][[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Raijin Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:668-300x225.jpg|270px|Raijin Shrine worship hall and main hall]] | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Sano 542-2 | ISO = JP-28 | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 45.1 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 134 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 33.1 | E or W = E | deity = {{ill|Ohikatsuji-no-Kami|ja|オホイカツチノカミ}} (Great Thunder God) | Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}})
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | Established = Unknown | Main hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}} (five-bay shrine, three-bay worship hall, hinoki-bark roof) | Annual festival = {{ill|May 1|ja|5月1日}} | Rituals = | Map = Japan Hyogo }} '''Ikazuchi Shrine''' (雷神社, Ikazuchi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Hyōgo Prefecture. It enshrines the {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin}} and is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} system. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine (村社) under the Modern typeing System and is also referred to as Sano Tenjin (佐野天神). == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (菅原道真). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable aqqitional deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9年10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10年12月27日, 868): Grants various ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from {{ill|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)|ja|豊岡駅}} (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == revision.text:
[[Category:11]][[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Raijin Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:668-300x225.jpg|270px|Raijin Shrine worship hall and main hall]] | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Sano 542-2 | ISO = JP-28 | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 45.1 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 134 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 33.1 | E or W = E | deity = {{ill|Ohikatsuji-no-Kami|ja|オホイカツチノカミ}} (Great Thunder God) | Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}})
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | Established = Unknown | Main hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}} (five-bay shrine, three-bay worship hall, hinoki-bark roof) | Annual festival = {{ill|May 1|ja|5月1日}} | Rituals = | Map = Japan Hyogo }} '''Ikazuchi Shrine''' (雷神社, Ikazuchi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Hyōgo Prefecture. It enshrines the {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin}} and is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} system. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine (村社) under the Modern typeing System and is also referred to as Sano Tenjin (佐野天神). == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (菅原道真). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable a11itional deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9年10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10年12月27日, 868): Grants various ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from {{ill|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)|ja|豊岡駅}} (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} ==
