revision.text:
[[Category:dd]][[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Raijin Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:668-300x225.jpg|270px|Raijin Shrine worship hall and main hall]] | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Sano 542-2 | ISO = JP-28 | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 45.1 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 134 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 33.1 | E or W = E | deity = {{ill|Ohikatsuji-no-Kami|ja|オホイカツチノカミ}} (Great Thunder God) | Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}})
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | Established = Unknown | Main hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}} (five-bay shrine, three-bay worship hall, hinoki-bark roof) | Annual festival = {{ill|May 1|ja|5月1日}} | Rituals = | Map = Japan Hyogo }} '''Ikazuchi Shrine''' (雷神社, Ikazuchi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Hyōgo Prefecture. It enshrines the {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin}} and is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} system. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine (村社) under the Modern typeing System and is also referred to as Sano Tenjin (佐野天神). == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable addition[[Category:addition]]al deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9年10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10年12月27日, 868): Grants various ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from {{ill|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)|ja|豊岡駅}} (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322053.html Ikazuchi Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Bureau) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ikazuchi Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier [[Category:dd]][[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Raijin Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:668-300x225.jpg|270px|Raijin Shrine worship hall and main hall]] | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Sano 542-2 | ISO = JP-28 | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 45.1 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 134 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 33.1 | E or W = E | deity = {{ill|Ohikatsuji-no-Kami|ja|オホイカツチノカミ}} (Great Thunder God) | Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}})
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | Established = Unknown | Main hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}} (five-bay shrine, three-bay worship hall, hinoki-bark roof) | Annual festival = {{ill|May 1|ja|5月1日}} | Rituals = | Map = Japan Hyogo }} '''Ikazuchi Shrine''' (雷神社, Ikazuchi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Hyōgo Prefecture. It enshrines the {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin}} and is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} system. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine (村社) under the Modern typeing System and is also referred to as Sano Tenjin (佐野天神). == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable addition[[Category:addition]]al deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9年10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10年12月27日, 868): Grants various ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from {{ill|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)|ja|豊岡駅}} (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322053.html Ikazuchi Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Bureau) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ikazuchi Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier [[Category:dd]][[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Raijin Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:668-300x225.jpg|270px|Raijin Shrine worship hall and main hall]] | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Sano 542-2 | ISO = JP-28 | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 45.1 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 134 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 33.1 | E or W = E | deity = {{ill|Ohikatsuji-no-Kami|ja|オホイカツチノカミ}} (Great Thunder God) | Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}})
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | Established = Unknown | Main hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}} (five-bay shrine, three-bay worship hall, hinoki-bark roof) | Annual festival = {{ill|May 1|ja|5月1日}} | Rituals = | Map = Japan Hyogo }} '''Ikazuchi Shrine''' (雷神社, Ikazuchi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Hyōgo Prefecture. It enshrines the {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin}} and is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} system. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine (村社) under the Modern typeing System and is also referred to as Sano Tenjin (佐野天神). == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable addition[[Category:addition]]al deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9年10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10年12月27日, 868): Grants various ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from {{ill|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)|ja|豊岡駅}} (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | Established = Unknown | Main hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}} (five-bay shrine, three-bay worship hall, hinoki-bark roof) | Annual festival = {{ill|May 1|ja|5月1日}} | Rituals = | Map = Japan Hyogo }} '''Ikazuchi Shrine''' (雷神社, Ikazuchi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Hyōgo Prefecture. It enshrines the {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin}} and is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} system. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine (村社) under the Modern typeing System and is also referred to as Sano Tenjin (佐野天神). == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable addition[[Category:addition]]al deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9年10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10年12月27日, 868): Grants various ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from {{ill|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)|ja|豊岡駅}} (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == [[Category:dd]][[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Raijin Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:668-300x225.jpg|270px|Raijin Shrine worship hall and main hall]] | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Sano 542-2 | ISO = JP-28 | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 45.1 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 134 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 33.1 | E or W = E | deity = {{ill|Ohikatsuji-no-Kami|ja|オホイカツチノカミ}} (Great Thunder God) | Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}})
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | Established = Unknown | Main hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}} (five-bay shrine, three-bay worship hall, hinoki-bark roof) | Annual festival = {{ill|May 1|ja|5月1日}} | Rituals = | Map = Japan Hyogo }} '''Ikazuchi Shrine''' (雷神社, Ikazuchi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Hyōgo Prefecture. It enshrines the {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin}} and is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} system. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine (村社) under the Modern typeing System and is also referred to as Sano Tenjin (佐野天神). == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable addition[[Category:addition]]al deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9年10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10年12月27日, 868): Grants various ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from {{ill|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)|ja|豊岡駅}} (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322053.html Ikazuchi Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Bureau) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ikazuchi Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier [[Category:dd]][[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Raijin Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:668-300x225.jpg|270px|Raijin Shrine worship hall and main hall]] | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Sano 542-2 | ISO = JP-28 | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 45.1 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 134 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 33.1 | E or W = E | deity = {{ill|Ohikatsuji-no-Kami|ja|オホイカツチノカミ}} (Great Thunder God) | Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}})
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | Established = Unknown | Main hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}} (five-bay shrine, three-bay worship hall, hinoki-bark roof) | Annual festival = {{ill|May 1|ja|5月1日}} | Rituals = | Map = Japan Hyogo }} '''Ikazuchi Shrine''' (雷神社, Ikazuchi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Hyōgo Prefecture. It enshrines the {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin}} and is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} system. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine (村社) under the Modern typeing System and is also referred to as Sano Tenjin (佐野天神). == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable addition[[Category:addition]]al deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9年10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10年12月27日, 868): Grants various ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from {{ill|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)|ja|豊岡駅}} (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地)== See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322053.html Ikazuchi Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Bureau) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ikazuchi Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier [[Category:dd]][[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Raijin Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:668-300x225.jpg|270px|Raijin Shrine worship hall and main hall]] | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Sano 542-2 | ISO = JP-28 | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 45.1 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 134 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 33.1 | E or W = E | deity = {{ill|Ohikatsuji-no-Kami|ja|オホイカツチノカミ}} (Great Thunder God) | Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}})
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | Established = Unknown | Main hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}} (five-bay shrine, three-bay worship hall, hinoki-bark roof) | Annual festival = {{ill|May 1|ja|5月1日}} | Rituals = | Map = Japan Hyogo }} '''Ikazuchi Shrine''' (雷神社, Ikazuchi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Hyōgo Prefecture. It enshrines the {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin}} and is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} system. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine (村社) under the Modern typeing System and is also referred to as Sano Tenjin (佐野天神). == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable addition[[Category:addition]]al deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9年10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10年12月27日, 868): Grants various ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from {{ill|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)|ja|豊岡駅}} (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == [[Category:dd]][[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Raijin Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:668-300x225.jpg|270px|Raijin Shrine worship hall and main hall]] | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Sano 542-2 | ISO = JP-28 | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 45.1 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 134 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 33.1 | E or W = E | deity = {{ill|Ohikatsuji-no-Kami|ja|オホイカツチノカミ}} (Great Thunder God) | Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}})
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | Established = Unknown | Main hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}} (five-bay shrine, three-bay worship hall, hinoki-bark roof) | Annual festival = {{ill|May 1|ja|5月1日}} | Rituals = | Map = Japan Hyogo }} '''Ikazuchi Shrine''' (雷神社, Ikazuchi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Hyōgo Prefecture. It enshrines the {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin}} and is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} system. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine (村社) under the Modern typeing System and is also referred to as Sano Tenjin (佐野天神). == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable addition[[Category:addition]]al deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9th year 10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10th year 12月27日, 868): Grants various ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from {{ill|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)|ja|豊岡駅}} (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from {{ill|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)|ja|豊岡駅}} (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == [[Category:dd]][[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Raijin Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:668-300x225.jpg|270px|Raijin Shrine worship hall and main hall]] | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Sano 542-2 | ISO = JP-28 | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 45.1 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 134 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 33.1 | E or W = E | deity = {{ill|Ohikatsuji-no-Kami|ja|オホイカツチノカミ}} (Great Thunder God) | Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}})
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | Established = Unknown | Main hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}} (five-bay shrine, three-bay worship hall, hinoki-bark roof) | Annual festival = {{ill|May 1|ja|5月1日}} | Rituals = | Map = Japan Hyogo }} '''Ikazuchi Shrine''' (雷神社, Ikazuchi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Hyōgo Prefecture. It enshrines the {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin}} and is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} system. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine (村社) under the Modern typeing System and is also referred to as Sano Tenjin (佐野天神). == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable addition[[Category:addition]]al deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9th year 10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10th year 12月27日, 868): Grants various ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from {{ill|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)|ja|豊岡駅}} (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Raijin Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:668-300x225.jpg|270px|Raijin Shrine worship hall and main hall]] | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Sano 542-2 | ISO = JP-28 | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 45.1 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 134 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 33.1 | E or W = E | deity = {{ill|Ohikatsuji-no-Kami|ja|オホイカツチノカミ}} (Great Thunder God) | Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}})
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} | Established = Unknown | Main hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}} (five-bay shrine, three-bay worship hall, hinoki-bark roof) | Annual festival = {{ill|May 1|ja|5月1日}} | Rituals = | Map = Japan Hyogo }} '''Ikazuchi Shrine''' (雷神社, Ikazuchi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市}}, Hyōgo Prefecture. It enshrines the {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin}} and is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} system. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine (村社) under the Modern typeing System and is also referred to as Sano Tenjin (佐野天神). == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable additional deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9th year 10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10th year 12月27日, 868): Grants various ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from {{ill|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)|ja|豊岡駅}} (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322053.html Ikazuchi Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Bureau) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| [[Category:dd]] [[Category:addition]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Prefectural Cultural Properties in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] [[sn:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[ja:雷神社 (豊岡市)]] [[sn:aijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[en:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ikazuchi Shrine}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Raijin Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:668-300x225.jpg|270px|Raijin Shrine worship hall and main hall]] | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市|12=draft}}, Sano 542-2 | ISO = JP-28 | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 45.1 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 134 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 33.1 | E or W = E | deity = {{ill|Ohikatsuji-no-Kami|ja|オホイカツチノカミ|12=draft}} (Great Thunder God) | Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft}})
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|12=draft}} | Established = Unknown | Main hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|12=draft}} (five-bay shrine, three-bay worship hall, hinoki-bark roof) | Annual festival = {{ill|May 1|ja|5月1日|12=draft}} | Rituals = | Map = Japan Hyogo }} '''Ikazuchi Shrine''' (雷神社, Ikazuchi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市|12=draft}}, Hyōgo Prefecture. It enshrines the {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin|12=draft}} and is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}} system. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine (村社) under the Modern typeing System and is also referred to as Sano Tenjin (佐野天神). == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable additional deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin|12=draft}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9th year 10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10th year 12月27日, 868): Grants various ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from {{ill|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)|ja|豊岡駅|12=draft}} (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港|12=draft}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社|12=draft}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322053.html Ikazuchi Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Bureau) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| [[Category:dd]] [[Category:addition]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Prefectural Cultural Properties in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] [[sn:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[ja:雷神社 (豊岡市)]] [[sn:aijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[en:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ikazuchi Shrine}}

''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港|12=draft}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社|12=draft}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322053.html Ikazuchi Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Bureau) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Prefectural Cultural Properties in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] [[sn:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[ja:雷神社 (豊岡市)]] [[sn:aijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[en:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ikazuchi Shrine}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Raijin Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:668-300x225.jpg|270px|Raijin Shrine worship hall and main hall]] | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市|12=draft}}, Sano 542-2 | ISO = JP-28 | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 45.1 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 134 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 33.1 | E or W = E | deity = {{ill|Ohikatsuji-no-Kami|ja|オホイカツチノカミ|12=draft}} (Great Thunder God) | Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft}})
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|12=draft}} | Established = Unknown | Main hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|12=draft}} (five-bay shrine, three-bay worship hall, hinoki-bark roof) | Annual festival = {{ill|May 1|ja|5月1日|12=draft}} | Rituals = | Map = Japan Hyogo }} '''Ikazuchi Shrine''' (雷神社, Ikazuchi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市|12=draft}}, Hyōgo Prefecture. It enshrines the {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin|12=draft}} and is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}} system. The shrine was formerly ranked as a village shrine (村社) under the Modern typeing System and is also referred to as Sano Tenjin (佐野天神). == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable additional deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin|12=draft}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9th year 10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10th year 12月27日, 868): Grants various ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from {{ill|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)|ja|豊岡駅|12=draft}} (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港|12=draft}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社|12=draft}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322053.html Ikazuchi Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Bureau) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Prefectural Cultural Properties in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] [[sn:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[ja:雷神社 (豊岡市)]] [[sn:aijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[en:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ikazuchi Shrine}}

Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable additional deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin|12=draft}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9th year 10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10th year 12月27日, 868): Grants various ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from {{ill|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)|ja|豊岡駅|12=draft}} (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港|12=draft}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社|12=draft}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322053.html Ikazuchi Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Bureau) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Prefectural Cultural Properties in Hyōgo Prefecture]] }} [[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] [[sn:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[ja:雷神社 (豊岡市)]] [[sn:aijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[en:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ikazuchi Shrine}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Raijin Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:668-300x225.jpg|270px|Raijin Shrine worship hall and main hall]] | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}, Sano 542-2 | ISO = JP-28 | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 45.1 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 134 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 33.1 | E or W = E | deity = {{ill|Ohikatsuji-no-Kami|ja|オホイカツチノカミ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} (Great Thunder God) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}})
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} | Established = Unknown | Main hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} (five-bay shrine, three-bay worship hall, hinoki-bark roof) | Annual festival = {{ill|May 1|ja|5月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} | Rituals = | Map = Japan Hyogo }} '''Ikazuchi Shrine''' (雷神社, Ikazuchi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}, Hyōgo Prefecture. It enshrines the {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} and is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} system. The shrine was formerly Ranked as a village shrine (村社) under the Modern typeing System and is also referred to as Sano Tenjin (佐野天神). == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable additional deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9th year 10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10th year 12月27日, 868): Grants various Ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from {{ill|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)|ja|豊岡駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322053.html Ikazuchi Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Bureau) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)|version=95849269|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Prefectural Cultural Properties in Hyōgo Prefecture]] }} [[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] [[sn:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[ja:雷神社 (豊岡市)]] [[sn:aijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[en:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ikazuchi Shrine}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Raijin Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:668-300x225.jpg|270px|Raijin Shrine worship hall and main hall]] | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Sano 542-2 | ISO = JP-28 | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 45.1 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 134 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 33.1 | E or W = E | deity = {{ill|Ohikatsuji-no-Kami|ja|オホイカツチノカミ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Great Thunder God) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}})
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Established = Unknown | Main hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (five-bay shrine, three-bay worship hall, hinoki-bark roof) | Annual festival = {{ill|May 1|ja|5月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Rituals = | Map = Japan Hyogo }} '''Ikazuchi Shrine''' (雷神社, Ikazuchi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Hyōgo Prefecture. It enshrines the {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} system. The shrine was formerly Ranked as a village shrine (村社) under the Modern typeing System and is also referred to as Sano Tenjin (佐野天神). == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable additional deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9th year 10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10th year 12月27日, 868): Grants various Ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from {{ill|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)|ja|豊岡駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322053.html Ikazuchi Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Bureau) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)|version=95849269|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Prefectural Cultural Properties in Hyōgo Prefecture]] }} [[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] [[sn:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[ja:雷神社 (豊岡市)]] [[sn:aijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[en:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Raijin Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:668-300x225.jpg|270px|Raijin Shrine worship hall and main hall]] | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Sano 542-2 | ISO = JP-28 | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 45.1 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 134 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 33.1 | E or W = E | deity = {{ill|Ohikatsuji-no-Kami|ja|オホイカツチノカミ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Great Thunder God) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}})
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Established = Unknown | Main hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (five-bay shrine, three-bay worship hall, hinoki-bark roof) | Annual festival = {{ill|May 1|ja|5月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Rituals = | Map = Japan Hyogo }} '''Ikazuchi Shrine''' (雷神社, Ikazuchi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Hyōgo Prefecture. It enshrines the {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} system. The shrine was formerly Ranked as a village shrine (村社) under the Modern typeing System and is also referred to as Sano Tenjin (佐野天神). == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable additional deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9th year 10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10th year 12月27日, 868): Grants various Ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from {{ill|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)|ja|豊岡駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322053.html Ikazuchi Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Bureau) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)|version=95849269|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Prefectural Cultural Properties in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] }} [[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] [[sn:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[ja:雷神社 (豊岡市)]] [[sn:aijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[en:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]]

The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable additional deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|qq=}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9th year 10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10th year 12月27日, 868): Grants various Ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from {{ill|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)|ja|豊岡駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)|qq=}} (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Airport|qq=}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaminomura Shrine|qq=}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322053.html Ikazuchi Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Bureau) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)|version=95849269|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Prefectural Cultural Properties in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] }} [[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] [[sn:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[ja:雷神社 (豊岡市)]] [[sn:aijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[en:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Raijin Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:668-300x225.jpg|270px|Raijin Shrine worship hall and main hall]] | location = [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Toyooka, Hyōgo|Toyooka, Hyōgo]], Sano 542-2 | ISO = JP-28 | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 45.1 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 134 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 33.1 | E or W = E | deity = {{ill|Ohikatsuji-no-Kami|ja|オホイカツチノカミ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohikatsuji-no-Kami|qq=}} (Great Thunder God) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=}})
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=}} | Established = Unknown | Main hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|qq=}} (five-bay shrine, three-bay worship hall, hinoki-bark roof) | Annual festival = [[:en:May 1|May 1]] | Rituals = | Map = Japan Hyōgo }} '''Ikazuchi Shrine''' (雷神社, Ikazuchi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Toyooka, Hyōgo|Toyooka, Hyōgo]], Hyōgo Prefecture. It enshrines the {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|qq=}} and is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=}} under the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] system. The shrine was formerly Ranked as a village shrine (村社) under the Modern typeing System and is also referred to as Sano Tenjin (佐野天神). == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable additional deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|qq=}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9th year 10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10th year 12月27日, 868): Grants various Ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from [[:en:Toyooka Station|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)]] (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Airport|qq=}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaminomura Shrine|qq=}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322053.html Ikazuchi Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Bureau) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)|version=95849269|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Prefectural Cultural Properties in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] }} [[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] [[sn:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[ja:雷神社 (豊岡市)]] [[sn:aijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[en:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyooka]] [[Category:豊岡市の歴史]]
