''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Airport|qq=}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaminomura Shrine|qq=}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322053.html Ikazuchi Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Bureau) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)|version=95849269|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Prefectural Cultural Properties in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] }} [[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] [[sn:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[ja:雷神社 (豊岡市)]] [[sn:aijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyooka]] [[Category:History of Toyooka]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Raijin Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:668-300x225.jpg|270px|Raijin Shrine worship hall and main hall]] | location = [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Toyooka, Hyōgo|Toyooka, Hyōgo]], Sano 542-2 | ISO = JP-28 | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 45.1 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 134 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 33.1 | E or W = E | deity = {{ill|Ohikatsuji-no-Kami|ja|オホイカツチノカミ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohikatsuji-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Great Thunder God) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}})
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | Established = Unknown | Main hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|qq=}} (five-bay shrine, three-bay worship hall, hinoki-bark roof) | Annual festival = [[:en:May 1|May 1]] | Rituals = | Map = Japan Hyōgo }} '''Ikazuchi Shrine''' (雷神社, Ikazuchi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Toyooka, Hyōgo|Toyooka, Hyōgo]], Hyōgo Prefecture. It enshrines the {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} under the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] system. The shrine was formerly Ranked as a village shrine (村社) under the Modern typeing System and is also referred to as Sano Tenjin (佐野天神). == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable additional deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9th year 10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10th year 12月27日, 868): Grants various Ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from [[:en:Toyooka Station|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)]] (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Airport|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaminomura Shrine|qq=}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322053.html Ikazuchi Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Bureau) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)|version=95849269|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Prefectural Cultural Properties in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] }} [[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] [[sn:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[ja:雷神社 (豊岡市)]] [[sn:aijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyooka]] [[Category:History of Toyooka]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Raijin Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:668-300x225.jpg|270px|Raijin Shrine worship hall and main hall]] | location = [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Toyooka, Hyōgo|Toyooka, Hyōgo]], Sano 542-2 | ISO = JP-28 | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 45.1 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 134 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 33.1 | E or W = E | deity = {{ill|Ohikatsuji-no-Kami|ja|オホイカツチノカミ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ohikatsuji-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Great Thunder God) | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}})
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | Established = Unknown | Main hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|qq=}} (five-bay shrine, three-bay worship hall, hinoki-bark roof) | Annual festival = [[:en:May 1|May 1]] | Rituals = | Map = Japan Hyōgo }} '''Ikazuchi Shrine''' (雷神社, Ikazuchi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Toyooka, Hyōgo|Toyooka, Hyōgo]], Hyōgo Prefecture. It enshrines the {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} under the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] system. The shrine was formerly Ranked as a village shrine (村社) under the Modern typeing System and is also referred to as Sano Tenjin (佐野天神). == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable additional deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9th year 10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10th year 12月27日, 868): Grants various Ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from [[:en:Toyooka Station|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)]] (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Airport|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaminomura Shrine|qq=}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322053.html Ikazuchi Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Bureau) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)|version=95849269|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] }} [[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] [[sn:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[ja:雷神社 (豊岡市)]] [[sn:aijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyooka]] [[Category:History of Toyooka]]

It enshrines the {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} under the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] system. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable additional deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9th year 10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10th year 12月27日, 868): Grants various Ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from [[:en:Toyooka Station|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)]] (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Airport|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaminomura Shrine|qq=}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322053.html Ikazuchi Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Bureau) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)|version=95849269|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] }} [[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] [[sn:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[ja:雷神社 (豊岡市)]] [[sn:aijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyooka]] [[Category:History of Toyooka]]

The shrine was formerly Ranked as a village shrine (村社) under the Modern typeing System and is also referred to as Sano Tenjin (佐野天神). == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable additional deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9th year 10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10th year 12月27日, 868): Grants various Ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from [[:en:Toyooka Station|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)]] (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Airport|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaminomura Shrine|qq=}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322053.html Ikazuchi Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Bureau) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)|version=95849269|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] }} [[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] [[sn:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[ja:雷神社 (豊岡市)]] [[sn:aijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyooka]] [[Category:History of Toyooka]]

''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Airport|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaminomura Shrine|qq=}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322053.html Ikazuchi Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Bureau) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)|version=95849269|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] }} [[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] [[sn:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[ja:雷神社 (豊岡市)]] [[sn:aijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyooka]] [[Category:History of Toyooka]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q21655264]] Wikidata name: Ikazuchi-Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Raijin Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:668-300x225.jpg | location = [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Toyooka, Hyōgo|Toyooka, Hyōgo]], Sano 542-2 | ISO = JP-28 | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 45.1 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 134 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 33.1 | E or W = E | deity = {{ill|Raijin|qid=Q12808082}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}})
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established = Unknown | Main hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|qq=|1=rake-overhanging gabled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13308575}} (five-bay shrine, three-bay worship hall, hinoki-bark roof) | Annual festival = [[:en:May 1|May 1]] | Rituals = | Map = Japan Hyōgo | location = {{ill|Toyooka|qid=Q696388}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.512528|N|134.809194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Ikazuchi Shrine''' (雷神社, Ikazuchi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Toyooka, Hyōgo|Toyooka, Hyōgo]], Hyōgo Prefecture. It enshrines the {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} under the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] system. The shrine was formerly Ranked as a village shrine (村社) under the Modern typeing System and is also referred to as Sano Tenjin (佐野天神). == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable additional deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9th year 10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10th year 12月27日, 868): Grants various Ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from [[:en:Toyooka Station|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)]] (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Airport|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaminomura Shrine|qq=}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322053.html Ikazuchi Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Bureau) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)|version=95849269|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] }} [[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] [[sn:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[ja:雷神社 (豊岡市)]] [[sn:aijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyooka]] [[Category:History of Toyooka]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q21655264]] Wikidata name: Ikazuchi-Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Raijin Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:668-300x225.jpg | location = [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Toyooka, Hyōgo|Toyooka, Hyōgo]], Sano 542-2 | ISO = JP-28 | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 45.1 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 134 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 33.1 | E or W = E | deity = {{ill|Raijin|qid=Q12808082}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}})
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established = Unknown | Main hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|qq=|1=rake-overhanging gabled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13308575}} (five-bay shrine, three-bay worship hall, hinoki-bark roof) | Annual festival = [[:en:May 1|May 1]] | Rituals = | Map = Japan Hyōgo | location = {{ill|Toyooka|qid=Q696388}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.512528|N|134.809194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Ikazuchi Shrine''' (雷神社, Ikazuchi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Toyooka, Hyōgo|Toyooka, Hyōgo]], Hyōgo Prefecture. It enshrines the {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} under the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] system. The shrine was formerly Ranked as a village shrine (村社) under the Modern typeing System and is also referred to as Sano Tenjin (佐野天神). == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable additional deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9th year 10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10th year 12月27日, 868): Grants various Ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from [[:en:Toyooka Station|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)]] (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Airport|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaminomura Shrine|qq=}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322053.html Ikazuchi Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Bureau) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)|version=95849269|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] }} [[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] [[sn:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[ja:雷神社 (豊岡市)]] [[sn:aijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyooka]] [[Category:History of Toyooka]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established = Unknown | Main hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|qq=|1=rake-overhanging gabled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13308575}} (five-bay shrine, three-bay worship hall, hinoki-bark roof) | Annual festival = [[:en:May 1|May 1]] | Rituals = | Map = Japan Hyōgo | location = {{ill|Toyooka|qid=Q696388}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.512528|N|134.809194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Ikazuchi Shrine''' (雷神社, Ikazuchi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Toyooka, Hyōgo|Toyooka, Hyōgo]], Hyōgo Prefecture. It enshrines the {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} under the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] system. The shrine was formerly Ranked as a village shrine (村社) under the Modern typeing System and is also referred to as Sano Tenjin (佐野天神). == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable additional deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9th year 10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10th year 12月27日, 868): Grants various Ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from [[:en:Toyooka Station|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)]] (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Airport|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaminomura Shrine|qq=}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322053.html Ikazuchi Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Bureau) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)|version=95849269|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] }} [[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] [[sn:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[ja:雷神社 (豊岡市)]] [[sn:aijin Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyooka]] [[Category:History of Toyooka]] [[sn:Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)]]

[[ja:雷神社 (豊岡市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q21655264]] Wikidata name: Ikazuchi-Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Raijin Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:668-300x225.jpg | location = [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Toyooka, Hyōgo|Toyooka, Hyōgo]], Sano 542-2 | ISO = JP-28 | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 45.1 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 134 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 33.1 | E or W = E | deity = {{ill|Raijin|qid=Q12808082}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}})
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established = Unknown | Main hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|qq=|1=rake-overhanging gabled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13308575}} (five-bay shrine, three-bay worship hall, hinoki-bark roof) | Annual festival = [[:en:May 1|May 1]] | Rituals = | Map = Japan Hyōgo | location = {{ill|Toyooka|qid=Q696388}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.512528|N|134.809194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Ikazuchi Shrine''' (雷神社, Ikazuchi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Toyooka, Hyōgo|Toyooka, Hyōgo]], Hyōgo Prefecture. It enshrines the {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} under the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] system. The shrine was formerly Ranked as a village shrine (村社) under the Modern typeing System and is also referred to as Sano Tenjin (佐野天神). == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable additional deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9th year 10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10th year 12月27日, 868): Grants various Ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from [[:en:Toyooka Station|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)]] (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Airport|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaminomura Shrine|qq=}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322053.html Ikazuchi Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Bureau) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)|version=95849269|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] }} [[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] [[ja:雷神社 (豊岡市)]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyooka]] [[Category:History of Toyooka]] {{wikidata link|Q21655264}}

[[ja:雷神社 (豊岡市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q21655264]] Wikidata name: Ikazuchi-Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Raijin Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:668-300x225.jpg | location = [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Toyooka, Hyōgo|Toyooka, Hyōgo]], Sano 542-2 | ISO = JP-28 | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 45.1 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 134 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 33.1 | E or W = E | deity = {{ill|Raijin|WD=Q12808082}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}})
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established = Unknown | Main hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|qq=|1=rake-overhanging gabled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13308575}} (five-bay shrine, three-bay worship hall, hinoki-bark roof) | Annual festival = [[:en:May 1|May 1]] | Rituals = | Map = Japan Hyōgo | location = {{ill|Toyooka|WD=Q696388}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.512528|N|134.809194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Ikazuchi Shrine''' (雷神社, Ikazuchi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Toyooka, Hyōgo|Toyooka, Hyōgo]], Hyōgo Prefecture. It enshrines the {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} under the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] system. The shrine was formerly Ranked as a village shrine (村社) under the Modern typeing System and is also referred to as Sano Tenjin (佐野天神). == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable additional deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9th year 10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10th year 12月27日, 868): Grants various Ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from [[:en:Toyooka Station|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)]] (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Airport|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaminomura Shrine|qq=}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322053.html Ikazuchi Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Bureau) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)|version=95849269|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] }} [[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] [[ja:雷神社 (豊岡市)]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyooka City]] [[Category:History of Toyooka]] {{wikidata link|Q21655264}}

[[ja:雷神社 (豊岡市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q21655264]] Wikidata name: Ikazuchi-Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Raijin Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:668-300x225.jpg | location = [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Toyooka, Hyōgo|Toyooka, Hyōgo]], Sano 542-2 | ISO = JP-28 | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 45.1 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 134 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 33.1 | E or W = E | deity = {{ill|Raijin|WD=Q12808082}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}})
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established = Unknown | Main hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|1=rake-overhanging gabled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13308575}} (five-bay shrine, three-bay worship hall, hinoki-bark roof) | Annual festival = [[:en:May 1|May 1]] | Rituals = | Map = Japan Hyōgo | location = {{ill|Toyooka|WD=Q696388}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.512528|N|134.809194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Ikazuchi Shrine''' (雷神社, Ikazuchi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Toyooka, Hyōgo|Toyooka, Hyōgo]], Hyōgo Prefecture. It enshrines the {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} under the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] system. The shrine was formerly Ranked as a village shrine (村社) under the Modern typeing System and is also referred to as Sano Tenjin (佐野天神). == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable additional deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9th year 10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10th year 12月27日, 868): Grants various Ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from [[:en:Toyooka Station|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)]] (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Airport|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaminomura Shrine|qq=}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322053.html Ikazuchi Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Bureau) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)|version=95849269|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] }} [[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] [[ja:雷神社 (豊岡市)]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyooka City]] [[Category:History of Toyooka]] {{wikidata link|Q21655264}}

Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established = Unknown | Main hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|1=rake-overhanging gabled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13308575}} (five-bay shrine, three-bay worship hall, hinoki-bark roof) | Annual festival = [[:en:May 1|May 1]] | Rituals = | Map = Japan Hyōgo | location = {{ill|Toyooka|WD=Q696388}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.512528|N|134.809194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Ikazuchi Shrine''' (雷神社, Ikazuchi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Toyooka, Hyōgo|Toyooka, Hyōgo]], Hyōgo Prefecture. It enshrines the {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} under the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] system. The shrine was formerly Ranked as a village shrine (村社) under the Modern typeing System and is also referred to as Sano Tenjin (佐野天神). == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable additional deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9th year 10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10th year 12月27日, 868): Grants various Ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from [[:en:Toyooka Station|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)]] (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Airport|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaminomura Shrine|qq=}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322053.html Ikazuchi Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Bureau) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)|version=95849269|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] }} [[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] [[ja:雷神社 (豊岡市)]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyooka City]] [[Category:History of Toyooka]] {{wikidata link|Q21655264}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

It enshrines the {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} under the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] system. The shrine was formerly Ranked as a village shrine (村社) under the Modern typeing System and is also referred to as Sano Tenjin (佐野天神). == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable additional deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9th year 10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10th year 12月27日, 868): Grants various Ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from [[:en:Toyooka Station|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)]] (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Airport|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaminomura Shrine|qq=}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322053.html Ikazuchi Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Bureau) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)|version=95849269|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] }} [[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] [[ja:雷神社 (豊岡市)]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyooka City]] [[Category:History of Toyooka]] {{wikidata link|Q21655264}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Airport|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaminomura Shrine|qq=}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322053.html Ikazuchi Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Bureau) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)|version=95849269|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] }} [[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] [[ja:雷神社 (豊岡市)]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyooka City]] [[Category:History of Toyooka]] {{wikidata link|Q21655264}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[ja:雷神社 (豊岡市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q21655264]] Wikidata name: Ikazuchi-Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Raijin Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:668-300x225.jpg | location = [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Toyooka, Hyōgo|Toyooka, Hyōgo]], Sano 542-2 | ISO = JP-28 | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 30 | Latitude seconds = 45.1 | N or S = N | Longitude degrees = 134 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 33.1 | E or W = E | deity = {{ill|Raijin|WD=Q12808082}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}})
Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established = Unknown | Main hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|1=rake-overhanging gabled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13308575}} (five-bay shrine, three-bay worship hall, hinoki-bark roof) | Annual festival = [[:en:May 1|May 1]] | Rituals = | Map = Japan Hyōgo | location = {{ill|Toyooka|WD=Q696388}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.512528|N|134.809194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Ikazuchi Shrine''' (雷神社, Ikazuchi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Toyooka, Hyōgo|Toyooka, Hyōgo]], Hyōgo Prefecture. It enshrines the {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} under the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] system. The shrine was formerly Ranked as a village shrine (村社) under the Modern typeing System and is also referred to as Sano Tenjin (佐野天神). == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable additional deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9th year 10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10th year 12月27日, 868): Grants various Ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from [[:en:Toyooka Station|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)]] (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Airport|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaminomura Shrine|qq=}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322053.html Ikazuchi Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Bureau) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)|version=95849269|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] }} [[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] [[ja:雷神社 (豊岡市)]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyooka City]] [[Category:History of Toyooka]] {{wikidata link|Q21655264}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established = Unknown | Main hall = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|1=rake-overhanging gabled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13308575}} (five-bay shrine, three-bay worship hall, hinoki-bark roof) | Annual festival = [[:en:May 1|May 1]] | Rituals = | Map = Japan Hyōgo | location = {{ill|Toyooka|WD=Q696388}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.512528|N|134.809194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Ikazuchi Shrine''' (雷神社, Ikazuchi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Toyooka, Hyōgo|Toyooka, Hyōgo]], Hyōgo Prefecture. It enshrines the {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} under the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] system. The shrine was formerly Ranked as a village shrine (村社) under the Modern typeing System and is also referred to as Sano Tenjin (佐野天神). == Overview == The exact date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. The shrine primarily enshrines the Ōikazuchi no Kami (大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin) as the main deity, along with Susanoo (須佐之男命) and Sugawara no Michizane (Sugawara no Michizane). Sugawara no Michizane, known for becoming Tenjin (the god of thunder) after his death, is a notable additional deity. == Deities == * {{ill|Ōikazuchi no Kami|ja|大雷神|lt=Ōikazuchi no Kami|3=雷神|1=Raijin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == Cultural Properties == * The shrine’s main hall (本殿) is designated as a Prefectural Cultural Property. == History == The shrine is mentioned in several historical records, including: * ''Shoku Nihon Kōki'' (承和9th year 10月乙亥, 842): Mentions the shrine as one of the official shrines of Tajima Province. * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' (貞観10th year 12月27日, 868): Grants various Ranks to shrines in Tajima Province, including Ikazuchi Shrine. * ''Engishiki'' (927): Lists Ikazuchi Shrine among the Myōjin Taisha of Tajima Province. According to the ''Kokushi Monjo Tajima Jinja Keifu-den'' (国司文書 但馬神社系譜伝), the shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Monmu (文武天皇) in response to severe mountain disasters affecting the regions of Tanba and Tajima. Offerings were made to pacify thunderous deities, and the shrine was founded on Sano Hill (狭沼丘) to pray for relief from lightning disasters. == Festivals == The shrine holds its annual festival on {{nihongo|May 1st|5月1日}}. == Access == ''By bus:'' * Approximately 24 minutes from [[:en:Toyooka Station|Toyooka Station (Hyōgo)]] (JR West San'in Main Line) by Zentan Bus, alighting at Naya Bus Stop, followed by a 1.4 km walk (23 minutes). ''By car:'' * Approximately 1.8 km northwest from the Tajima Airport entrance intersection on National Route 312. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tajima Airport|ja|こうのとり但馬空港|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Airport|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Toyooka Bag District (豊岡鞄団地) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|ja|但馬国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Tajima Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Kaminomura Shrine|ja|上之村神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaminomura Shrine|qq=}} (also known as Kaminomura Raiden Shrine). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322053.html Ikazuchi Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Bureau) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)|version=95849269|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:雷神社_(豊岡市)]] [[ja:雷神社 (豊岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q21655264}} [[Category:Buildings in Toyooka City]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Toyooka]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
